root_text.sn48.69:1.1: “Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, ye keci asurānaṁ rukkhā, cittapāṭali tesaṁ aggamakkhāyati;
root_text.sn48.69:1.2: evameva kho, bhikkhave, ye keci bodhipakkhiyā dhammā, paññindriyaṁ tesaṁ aggamakkhāyati, yadidaṁ—bodhāya.
root_text.sn48.69:1.4: Saddhindriyaṁ, bhikkhave, bodhipakkhiyo dhammo, taṁ bodhāya saṁvattati …pe… paññindriyaṁ bodhipakkhiyo dhammo, taṁ bodhāya saṁvattati.
root_text.sn48.69:1.5: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, ye keci asurānaṁ rukkhā, cittapāṭali tesaṁ aggamakkhāyati;
root_text.sn48.69:1.6: evameva kho, bhikkhave, ye keci bodhipakkhiyā dhammā, paññindriyaṁ tesaṁ aggamakkhāyati, yadidaṁ—bodhāyā”ti.

translation_text.sn48.69:1.1: “Mendicants, of all the trees belonging to the titans, the vivid patala is said to be the best.
translation_text.sn48.69:1.2: In the same way, the faculty of wisdom is said to be the best of the qualities that lead to awakening in terms of becoming awakened.
translation_text.sn48.69:1.3: And what are the qualities that lead to awakening?
translation_text.sn48.69:1.4: The faculties of faith, energy, mindfulness, immersion, and wisdom are qualities that lead to awakening, in that they lead to becoming awakened.
translation_text.sn48.69:1.5: Of all the trees belonging to the titans, the vivid patala is said to be the best.
translation_text.sn48.69:1.6: In the same way, the faculty of wisdom is said to be the best of the qualities that lead to awakening in terms of becoming awakened.”
